title: Model Router
description: An intelligent LLM model router that selects the optimal provider and model
based on configurable routing strategies. Supports complexity-based routing
(simple queries to cheap models, complex to powerful ones), cost-aware routing
(switches to cheaper models when budget threshold is hit), latency-optimized
routing, ordered fallback chains, and random A/B traffic splitting for model

input.description: Incoming questions to be routed to the optimal model

output.description: Questions annotated with routing metadata (provider, model, tier, reason)

router.strategy.title: Routing strategy
router.strategy.description: The routing strategy to use for model selection

router.primary_model.title: Primary model
router.primary_model.description: The primary model to route to (e.g. claude-sonnet, gpt-5, gemini-pro)

router.fallback_models.title: Fallback models
router.fallback_models.description: Comma-separated list of fallback models (e.g. claude-haiku,gpt-5-nano)

router.budget_limit.title: Budget limit ($)
router.budget_limit.description: Maximum cumulative cost before switching to cheaper models (0 = unlimited)

router.ab_split_percent.title: A/B split percentage
router.ab_split_percent.description: Percentage of traffic routed to group A (primary model). Remainder goes to group B (first fallback).

router.complexity_threshold.title: Complexity threshold
router.complexity_threshold.description: Score threshold above which queries are considered complex and routed to powerful models
